books.title: Paradise Lost
books.author: John Milton
books.summary: The epic poem of humanity's fall from grace, featuring Satan's rebellion against God.
books.historical_context: Written during the English Restoration, reflecting religious and political tensions of the time.

books.title: The Book of the Dead
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: Collection of spells and instructions for the journey through the afterlife.
books.historical_context: Developed over centuries in ancient Egypt, fundamental to Egyptian religious beliefs.

books.title: The Divine Comedy
books.author: Dante Alighieri
books.summary: Allegorical journey through the afterlife realms of Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise.
books.historical_context: Written during Dante's exile from Florence, reflecting medieval Christian theology and Italian politics.

books.title: The Canterbury Tales
books.author: Geoffrey Chaucer
books.summary: A collection of stories told by pilgrims traveling to Canterbury Cathedral, providing a vivid portrait of medieval English society.
books.historical_context: Written during the late Middle Ages, depicting the social dynamics and cultural values of medieval England across different social classes.

books.title: The Iliad
books.author: Homer
books.summary: The epic tale of the Trojan War, focusing on the wrath of Achilles and its consequences.
books.historical_context: Composed in the 8th century BCE, reflecting Bronze Age warfare and Greek mythology.

books.title: The Odyssey
books.author: Homer
books.summary: Odysseus's ten-year journey home after the Trojan War, facing mythical creatures and divine intervention.
books.historical_context: Composed alongside The Iliad, depicting Greek maritime culture and the importance of hospitality in ancient society.

books.title: The Aeneid
books.author: Virgil
books.summary: The journey of Aeneas from Troy to Italy, where he becomes the ancestor of the Romans.
books.historical_context: Written during Augustus's reign, establishing a mythical connection between Troy and Rome.

books.title: Gulliver's Travels
books.author: Jonathan Swift
books.summary: Lemuel Gulliver's voyages to various strange lands, satirizing human nature and society.
books.historical_context: Written as a satire of travel narratives and contemporary European politics.

books.title: The Decameron
books.author: Giovanni Boccaccio
books.summary: A collection of 100 tales told by ten young people sheltering from the Black Death.
books.historical_context: Written during the aftermath of the Black Death, reflecting medieval Italian society and values.

books.title: The Tale of the Heike
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: The epic account of the struggle between the Taira and Minamoto clans for control of Japan.
books.historical_context: Chronicles the Genpei War (1180-1185), marking the end of the classical period in Japanese history.

books.title: Dead Souls
books.author: Nikolai Gogol
books.summary: Chichikov's scheme to buy deceased serfs' names from landowners, revealing the absurdities of Russian society.
books.historical_context: Written during the reform period of Nicholas I, satirizing Russian serfdom and bureaucracy.

books.title: The Master and Margarita
books.author: Mikhail Bulgakov
books.summary: Satan visits Soviet Moscow, interweaving a love story with a satirical critique of Soviet society.
books.historical_context: Written during Stalin's regime, published posthumously, reflecting the absurdities of Soviet life.

books.title: The Man Without Qualities
books.author: Robert Musil
books.summary: Ulrich's search for meaning in the last days of the Austro-Hungarian Empire.
books.historical_context: Set before World War I, examining the decline of the Habsburg Empire and modern alienation.

books.title: The Life and Opinions of Tristram Shandy
books.author: Laurence Sterne
books.summary: The rambling, digressive autobiography of Tristram Shandy, who cannot even manage to get himself born.
books.historical_context: Written during the Age of Enlightenment, experimenting with narrative conventions.

books.title: Fathers and Sons
books.author: Ivan Turgenev
books.summary: The conflict between generations and ideologies in 1850s Russia through the story of nihilist Bazarov.
books.historical_context: Written during the period of great reforms in Russia, examining the generational divide.

books.title: The Tin Drum
books.author: Günter Grass
books.summary: Oskar Matzerath, who decides not to grow up, witnesses the rise and fall of Nazi Germany.
books.historical_context: Written in post-war Germany, examining the Nazi period through magical realism.

books.title: The Recognition of Śakuntalā
books.author: Kālidāsa
books.summary: The love story of King Dushyanta and Shakuntala, separated by a curse and reunited through a ring.
books.historical_context: Written during India's classical period, exemplifying Sanskrit dramatic traditions.

books.title: The Dream of the Red Chamber
books.author: Cao Xueqin
books.summary: The rise and fall of the Jia family and the love story between Jia Baoyu and Lin Daiyu.
books.historical_context: Written during the Qing Dynasty, depicting the decline of noble families.

books.title: Season of Migration to the North
books.author: Tayeb Salih
books.summary: A Sudanese man's experiences in England and his return home, exploring colonialism and identity.
books.historical_context: Written post-independence, examining the complex relationship between Africa and Europe.

books.title: The Golden Notebook
books.author: Doris Lessing
books.summary: Writer Anna Wulf's attempt to integrate different aspects of her life through four notebooks.
books.historical_context: Written during the Cold War, exploring feminism, communism, and mental breakdown.

books.title: Death Comes for the Archbishop
books.author: Willa Cather
books.summary: The life of a French Catholic priest building a diocese in the American Southwest.
books.historical_context: Set in the mid-19th century, depicting the American frontier and cultural encounters.

books.title: The Plague
books.author: Albert Camus
books.summary: A city's response to a deadly plague, examining human resilience and solidarity.
books.historical_context: Written after World War II, using plague as an allegory for the Nazi occupation.

books.title: The Master of Go
books.author: Yasunari Kawabata
books.summary: A championship Go match between an old master and a modern challenger.
books.historical_context: Based on a real 1938 match, symbolizing the clash between tradition and modernity in Japan.

books.title: Austerlitz
books.author: W.G. Sebald
books.summary: An architectural historian discovers his Jewish origins and childhood escape from Nazi Germany.
books.historical_context: Written at the turn of the millennium, examining Holocaust memory and European history.

books.title: The Leopard
books.author: Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa
books.summary: A Sicilian aristocrat witnesses the decline of his class during Italian unification.
books.historical_context: Set during the Risorgimento, examining social change in 19th century Italy.

books.title: The Tale of Kieu
books.author: Nguyễn Du
books.summary: A virtuous woman's sacrifices and suffering to save her family, written in verse.
books.historical_context: Written during the Nguyen dynasty, reflecting Vietnamese cultural values and Chinese influence.

books.title: Pedro Páramo
books.author: Juan Rulfo
books.summary: A man's search for his father in a ghost town populated by spirits of the dead.
books.historical_context: Written after the Mexican Revolution, examining power, death, and Mexican identity.

books.title: The Complete Stories of Flannery O'Connor
books.author: Flannery O'Connor
books.summary: Collection of stories exploring faith, morality, and the grotesque in the American South.
books.historical_context: Written during the Civil Rights era, examining religious faith and racial tensions.

books.title: The Book of Disquiet
books.author: Fernando Pessoa
books.summary: The fragmentary thoughts and observations of Bernardo Soares, a bookkeeper in Lisbon.
books.historical_context: Written during Portugal's transition from monarchy to dictatorship, exploring modern alienation.

books.title: Blood Meridian
books.author: Cormac McCarthy
books.summary: A violent journey through the American-Mexican borderlands in the 1850s.
books.historical_context: Based on historical events, examining the violence of American westward expansion.

books.title: The Mahabharata
books.author: Vyasa
books.summary: Epic tale of the Kurukshetra War and its aftermath, exploring dharma and moral duty.
books.historical_context: Composed over centuries, fundamental to Hindu philosophy and Indian culture.

books.title: The Possessed
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: A group of revolutionaries bring chaos to a small Russian town.
books.historical_context: Written in response to real revolutionary movements in Russia, examining nihilism and political radicalism.

books.title: The Makioka Sisters
books.author: Junichiro Tanizaki
books.summary: The decline of an aristocratic Osaka family through the lives of four sisters.
books.historical_context: Set before World War II, depicting the conflict between tradition and modernity in Japan.

books.title: If on a winter's night a traveler
books.author: Italo Calvino
books.summary: A novel about reading novels, where the reader becomes a character in the story.
books.historical_context: Written during postmodernism's height, experimenting with narrative structure and reader involvement.

books.title: Ulysses
books.author: James Joyce
books.summary: A modernist novel following Leopold Bloom through a single day in Dublin, paralleling Homer's Odyssey.
books.historical_context: Written during the Irish struggle for independence, pioneering stream-of-consciousness narrative.

books.title: In Search of Lost Time
books.author: Marcel Proust
books.summary: A massive novel exploring memory, time, and French society through the narrator's recollections.
books.historical_context: Written during the Belle Époque, examining the decline of aristocratic French society.

books.title: The Catcher in the Rye
books.author: J. D. Salinger
books.summary: Holden Caulfield's journey through New York City, exploring teenage alienation and phoniness.
books.historical_context: Written post-WWII, capturing American youth disillusionment in the 1950s.

books.title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
books.author: George Orwell
books.summary: A dystopian novel about totalitarian control, surveillance, and the manipulation of truth.
books.historical_context: Written in the aftermath of WWII, warning against totalitarian regimes and propaganda.

books.title: Anna Karenina
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.summary: The tragic story of Anna Karenina's affair and its consequences in 19th-century Russian society.
books.historical_context: Written during Russian social reforms, examining the tensions between tradition and modernity.

books.title: Wuthering Heights
books.author: Emily Brontë
books.summary: A dark tale of passion and revenge between Heathcliff and Catherine on the Yorkshire moors.
books.historical_context: Written during the Victorian era, challenging social conventions with its gothic intensity.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: Raskolnikov's psychological journey after committing murder, exploring guilt and redemption.
books.historical_context: Written during Russian social upheaval, examining moral philosophy and human psychology.

books.title: Madame Bovary
books.author: Gustave Flaubert
books.summary: Emma Bovary's pursuit of romantic ideals leads to tragedy in provincial French society.
books.historical_context: Written during the Second Empire, critiquing bourgeois society and romantic illusions.

books.title: The Brothers Karamazov
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: A philosophical novel about faith, morality, and family through the story of three brothers.
books.historical_context: Dostoevsky's final novel, exploring Russian Orthodox faith and moral questions.

books.title: The Trial
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.summary: Josef K.'s nightmarish experience with an incomprehensible legal system.
books.historical_context: Published posthumously, reflecting the bureaucratic absurdity of modern society.

books.title: To the Lighthouse
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.summary: A modernist exploration of time, memory, and human relationships centered around the Ramsay family.
books.historical_context: Written between the wars, pioneering stream-of-consciousness technique.

books.title: Heart of Darkness
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: Marlow's journey into the African interior reveals the darkness of colonialism and human nature.
books.historical_context: Written during the height of European colonialism, exposing its brutal realities.

books.title: The Grapes of Wrath
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.summary: The Joad family's migration to California during the Great Depression.
books.historical_context: Written during the Dust Bowl era, depicting the struggles of migrant workers.

books.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
books.author: Lewis Carroll
books.summary: Alice's fantastical journey through a world of nonsense and imagination.
books.historical_context: Written during the Victorian era, revolutionizing children's literature.

books.title: The Magic Mountain
books.author: Thomas Mann
books.summary: Hans Castorp's transformative stay at a tuberculosis sanatorium in the Swiss Alps.
books.historical_context: Written in the aftermath of WWI, exploring European intellectual and cultural crisis.

books.title: Middlemarch
books.author: George Eliot
books.summary: A panoramic view of provincial English life through interconnected stories of love, politics, and idealism.
books.historical_context: Set during the Reform Bill era, examining social and political change in England.

books.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: Huck Finn's journey down the Mississippi River with runaway slave Jim.
books.historical_context: Written post-Civil War, addressing issues of race and freedom in American society.

books.title: Mrs. Dalloway
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.summary: A single day in the life of Clarissa Dalloway, exploring consciousness and post-war trauma.
books.historical_context: Written in the aftermath of WWI, examining shell shock and social change.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë
books.summary: An orphaned governess's journey to independence and love despite social constraints.
books.historical_context: Written during the Victorian era, challenging gender roles and class structures.

books.title: Catch-22
books.author: Joseph Heller
books.summary: Captain Yossarian's absurd experiences in WWII, trapped by military bureaucracy.
books.historical_context: Written during the Cold War, satirizing military logic and bureaucratic absurdity.

books.title: Great Expectations
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: Pip's journey from humble origins to gentleman, exploring ambition and moral growth.
books.historical_context: Written during Victorian England's industrial expansion and social mobility.

books.title: The Master and Margarita
books.author: Mikhail Bulgakov
books.summary: Satan visits Soviet Moscow in this satirical blend of fantasy and social criticism.
books.historical_context: Written during Stalin's era but published later, critiquing Soviet society.

books.title: Invisible Man
books.author: Ralph Ellison
books.summary: An unnamed Black narrator's journey from the South to Harlem, exploring racial identity and invisibility.
books.historical_context: Written during the Civil Rights era, examining African American experience and identity.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.summary: Victor Frankenstein's creation of life and the monster's quest for acceptance and revenge.
books.historical_context: Written during the Romantic period, exploring scientific ethics and human nature.

books.title: The Sun Also Rises
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.summary: The Lost Generation's disillusionment in post-WWI Paris and Spain.
books.historical_context: Written about the aftermath of WWI, defining the 'Lost Generation' of expatriates.

books.title: Les Misérables
books.author: Victor Hugo
books.summary: Jean Valjean's redemption story set against the backdrop of 19th-century French society.
books.historical_context: Written during the Second Empire, spanning French history from Napoleon to the July Revolution.

books.title: On the Road
books.author: Jack Kerouac
books.summary: Sal Paradise's cross-country journeys with Dean Moriarty, embodying Beat Generation freedom.
books.historical_context: Written during the conformist 1950s, celebrating counterculture and spontaneity.

books.title: Absalom, Absalom!
books.author: William Faulkner
books.summary: The rise and fall of Thomas Sutpen's dynasty in the antebellum South.
books.historical_context: Written during the Depression, examining the legacy of slavery and the Old South.

books.title: Midnight's Children
books.author: Salman Rushdie
books.summary: Saleem Sinai's life parallels India's independence and partition through magical realism.
books.historical_context: Written decades after Partition, examining post-colonial identity and history.

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.author: Chinua Achebe
books.summary: Okonkwo's tragic story as traditional Igbo society encounters European colonialism.
books.historical_context: Written during African decolonization, portraying pre-colonial African society.

books.title: The Little Prince
books.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
books.summary: A young prince's journey across planets, exploring human nature through a child's eyes.
books.historical_context: Written during WWII exile, offering hope and wisdom through allegory.

books.title: Journey to the End of the Night
books.author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline
books.summary: Ferdinand Bardamu's cynical journey through war, colonialism, and urban decay.
books.historical_context: Written between the wars, expressing disillusionment with modern civilization.

books.title: The Red and the Black
books.author: Stendhal
books.summary: Julien Sorel's ambitious climb through French society ends in tragedy.
books.historical_context: Written during the Restoration, critiquing social climbing and hypocrisy.

books.title: Fictions
books.author: Jorge Luis Borges
books.summary: A collection of philosophical short stories exploring labyrinths, infinity, and reality.
books.historical_context: Written during WWII, influencing postmodern literature with metafictional techniques.

books.title: David Copperfield
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: David's autobiographical journey from childhood to maturity in Victorian England.
books.historical_context: Dickens' most personal novel, depicting Victorian society and social reform.

books.title: Brave New World
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.summary: A dystopian future where happiness is engineered and individuality is suppressed.
books.historical_context: Written during the rise of totalitarianism, warning against technological control.

books.title: Animal Farm
books.author: George Orwell
books.summary: Farm animals rebel against their human owner, but their revolution becomes corrupted.
books.historical_context: An allegory of the Russian Revolution and Stalin's rise to power.

books.title: The Old Man and the Sea
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.summary: An aging fisherman's epic struggle with a giant marlin in the Gulf Stream.
books.historical_context: Written late in Hemingway's career, embodying his philosophy of grace under pressure.

books.title: The Life and Opinions of Tristram Shandy
books.author: Laurence Sterne
books.summary: A wildly digressive autobiography that barely gets to the narrator's birth.
books.historical_context: An experimental novel that influenced modernist literature centuries later.

books.title: The Diary of a Young Girl
books.author: Anne Frank
books.summary: Anne Frank's diary documenting her life in hiding during the Nazi occupation.
books.historical_context: A firsthand account of the Holocaust, published posthumously as a testament to hope.

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.summary: The March sisters growing up during the Civil War, exploring family bonds and female independence.
books.historical_context: Written during Reconstruction, promoting women's education and moral development.

books.title: Hamlet
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: Prince Hamlet's quest for revenge against his uncle who murdered his father.
books.historical_context: Written during the Elizabethan era, exploring themes of revenge, madness, and mortality.

books.title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
books.author: Zora Neale Hurston
books.summary: Janie Crawford's journey of self-discovery and love in the American South.
books.historical_context: Written during the Harlem Renaissance, celebrating African American culture and female empowerment.

books.title: Rebecca
books.author: Daphne du Maurier
books.summary: A young bride haunted by the memory of her husband's deceased first wife.
books.historical_context: Written on the eve of WWII, exploring themes of identity and the past's influence.

books.title: Gone with the Wind
books.author: Margaret Mitchell
books.summary: Scarlett O'Hara's survival and determination during the Civil War and Reconstruction.
books.historical_context: Written during the Depression, romanticizing the antebellum South.

books.title: Gulliver's Travels
books.author: Jonathan Swift
books.summary: Lemuel Gulliver's voyages to strange lands, satirizing human nature and society.
books.historical_context: Written during the Enlightenment, using satire to critique politics and human folly.

books.title: Slaughterhouse-Five
books.author: Kurt Vonnegut
books.summary: Billy Pilgrim's experiences in WWII and his time-traveling encounters with aliens.
books.historical_context: Written during the Vietnam War, using dark humor to address war's trauma.

books.title: A Passage to India
books.author: E. M. Forster
books.summary: The complex relationships between British colonizers and Indians during the Raj.
books.historical_context: Written during the independence movement, examining colonial tensions and misunderstandings.

books.title: Lord of the Flies
books.author: William Golding
books.summary: British schoolboys stranded on an island descend into savagery.
books.historical_context: Written post-WWII, exploring the darkness of human nature and civilization's fragility.

books.title: Candide
books.author: Voltaire
books.summary: Candide's journey through a series of misfortunes, satirizing philosophical optimism.
books.historical_context: Written during the Enlightenment, critiquing blind optimism and social institutions.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.summary: Paul Atreides' rise to power on the desert planet Arrakis in a galactic empire.
books.historical_context: Written during the space race, blending ecology, politics, and mysticism.

books.title: The Handmaid's Tale
books.author: Margaret Atwood
books.summary: A dystopian future where fertile women are enslaved for reproduction.
books.historical_context: Written during the Reagan era, warning against religious fundamentalism and women's rights erosion.

books.title: Leaves of Grass
books.author: Walt Whitman
books.summary: A revolutionary collection of poems celebrating democracy, nature, and the human spirit.
books.historical_context: Written during American expansion, defining a uniquely American poetic voice.

books.title: The Good Soldier
books.author: Ford Madox Ford
books.summary: The tragic relationships between two couples, told through an unreliable narrator.
books.historical_context: Written on the eve of WWI, examining the collapse of Edwardian society.

books.title: The Leopard
books.author: Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa
books.summary: A Sicilian prince witnesses the decline of aristocracy during Italian unification.
books.historical_context: Set during the Risorgimento, examining social transformation in 19th-century Italy.

books.title: Orlando
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.summary: An immortal character who changes gender across four centuries of English history.
books.historical_context: Written during the suffrage movement, exploring gender fluidity and literary history.

books.title: The Portrait of a Lady
books.author: Henry James
books.summary: Isabel Archer's quest for independence and her tragic marriage in European society.
books.historical_context: Written during the Gilded Age, examining American innocence versus European sophistication.

books.title: The Golden Notebook
books.author: Doris Lessing
books.summary: Anna Wulf's attempt to integrate her fragmented life through four notebooks.
books.historical_context: Written during the women's liberation movement, exploring female consciousness and political commitment.

books.title: Essays
books.author: Michel de Montaigne
books.summary: Personal reflections on human nature, creating the essay as a literary form.
books.historical_context: Written during the French Renaissance, pioneering introspective writing.

books.title: Dracula
books.author: Bram Stoker
books.summary: Count Dracula's invasion of England and the battle to stop him.
books.historical_context: Written during the height of the British Empire, reflecting fears of foreign invasion and moral decay.

books.title: Oedipus the King
books.author: Sophocles
books.summary: King Oedipus discovers he has fulfilled a prophecy by killing his father and marrying his mother.
books.historical_context: A foundational Greek tragedy exploring fate, knowledge, and human hubris.

books.title: The Idiot
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: Prince Myshkin's Christ-like innocence clashes with corrupt Russian society.
books.historical_context: Written during Russian social upheaval, exploring the possibility of goodness in a fallen world.

books.title: The Castle
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.summary: K.'s futile attempts to gain access to the mysterious Castle and its authorities.
books.historical_context: Published posthumously, representing the alienation of modern bureaucratic society.

books.title: The Metamorphosis
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.summary: Gregor Samsa wakes up transformed into a giant insect, exploring alienation and family duty.
books.historical_context: Written during WWI, embodying the anxiety and dehumanization of modern life.

books.title: Buddenbrooks
books.author: Thomas Mann
books.summary: The decline of a merchant family over four generations in 19th-century Germany.
books.historical_context: Written at the turn of the century, examining the end of the bourgeois era.

books.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.summary: A young man's portrait ages while he remains beautiful, leading to moral corruption.
books.historical_context: Written during the aesthetic movement, exploring art, beauty, and moral decay.

books.title: In Cold Blood
books.author: Truman Capote
books.summary: The true story of a family murder in rural Kansas, pioneering the non-fiction novel.
books.historical_context: Written during the 1960s, exploring violence in small-town America.

books.title: All Quiet on the Western Front
books.author: Erich Maria Remarque
books.summary: Young German soldiers experience the horrors and futility of World War I.
books.historical_context: Written during the interwar period, presenting an anti-war perspective on WWI.

books.title: The Age of Innocence
books.author: Edith Wharton
books.summary: Newland Archer's love for Ellen Olenska conflicts with New York society's expectations.
books.historical_context: A retrospective look at 1870s New York society from the perspective of the 1920s.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.summary: Edmond Dantès' elaborate revenge against those who wrongfully imprisoned him.
books.historical_context: Set during the Napoleonic era and Restoration, exploring themes of justice and revenge.

books.title: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man
books.author: James Joyce
books.summary: Stephen Dedalus's artistic and intellectual development in early 20th-century Ireland.
books.historical_context: Written during Irish nationalism's rise, exploring the conflict between art and politics.

books.title: Faust
books.author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
books.summary: Dr. Faust's pact with the devil in his quest for knowledge and experience.
books.historical_context: Goethe's masterwork spanning decades, embodying Romantic ideals and German culture.

books.title: Under the Volcano
books.author: Malcolm Lowry
books.summary: Geoffrey Firmin's final day of alcoholic dissolution on the Day of the Dead.
books.historical_context: Written in the shadow of WWII, exploring personal and political destruction.

books.title: As I Lay Dying
books.author: William Faulkner
books.summary: The Bundren family's journey to bury their matriarch, told through multiple perspectives.
books.historical_context: Written during the Depression, exploring Southern poverty and family bonds.

books.title: The Scarlet Letter
books.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
books.summary: Hester Prynne's public shaming for adultery in Puritan New England.
books.historical_context: Written during the antebellum period, critiquing Puritan society and exploring sin and redemption.

books.title: Emma
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: Emma Woodhouse's misguided matchmaking in her small English village.
books.historical_context: Written during the Regency period, satirizing social conventions and female education.

books.title: Silent Spring
books.author: Rachel Carson
books.summary: A groundbreaking examination of pesticides' environmental impact.
books.historical_context: Launched the modern environmental movement and led to DDT's ban.

books.title: Pale Fire
books.author: Vladimir Nabokov
books.summary: A 999-line poem with an elaborate commentary revealing the commentator's delusions.
books.historical_context: Written during the Cold War, parodying academic criticism and exploring exile.

books.title: Charlotte's Web
books.author: E. B. White
books.summary: A spider named Charlotte saves her friend Wilbur the pig from slaughter.
books.historical_context: A beloved children's story exploring friendship, sacrifice, and the cycle of life.

books.title: Demons
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: Revolutionary conspirators bring chaos to a Russian town, exploring political radicalism.
books.historical_context: Based on real revolutionary activities, examining the destructive nature of radical politics.

books.title: Robinson Crusoe
books.author: Daniel Defoe
books.summary: A shipwrecked sailor survives alone on a tropical island for decades.
books.historical_context: Often considered the first English novel, reflecting colonial attitudes and individual resilience.

books.title: The Bell Jar
books.author: Sylvia Plath
books.summary: Esther Greenwood's descent into mental illness in 1950s America.
books.historical_context: A semi-autobiographical novel exploring women's roles and mental health in the 1950s.

books.title: Waiting for Godot
books.author: Samuel Beckett
books.summary: Two tramps wait endlessly for someone named Godot who never arrives.
books.historical_context: Written post-WWII, defining Theater of the Absurd and existential uncertainty.

books.title: The Complete Tales and Poems of Edgar Allan Poe
books.author: Edgar Allan Poe
books.summary: A collection of macabre tales and poems exploring death, madness, and the supernatural.
books.historical_context: Poe's works defined American Gothic literature and the modern short story.

books.title: The Plague
books.author: Albert Camus
books.summary: A city's response to a deadly plague, exploring human solidarity and absurdity.
books.historical_context: An allegory for the Nazi occupation of France and the human condition.

books.title: Native Son
books.author: Richard Wright
books.summary: Bigger Thomas's life in Chicago's South Side leads to violence and tragedy.
books.historical_context: Written during the Great Migration, exposing urban racism and poverty.

books.title: Vanity Fair
books.author: William Makepeace Thackeray
books.summary: Becky Sharp's ambitious climb through society during the Napoleonic Wars.
books.historical_context: A satirical 'novel without a hero' critiquing early Victorian society.

books.title: The Stories of Anton Chekhov
books.author: Anton Chekhov
books.summary: Masterful short stories capturing the melancholy of Russian life.
books.historical_context: Written during Russia's transition period, perfecting the modern short story form.

books.title: The Color Purple
books.author: Alice Walker
books.summary: Celie's journey from abuse to empowerment in the rural South.
books.historical_context: Written during the women's rights movement, addressing racism and sexism.

books.title: Walden
books.author: Henry David Thoreau
books.summary: Thoreau's experiment in simple living by Walden Pond.
books.historical_context: A founding text of environmentalism and civil disobedience.

books.title: The Man Without Qualities
books.author: Robert Musil
books.summary: Ulrich's search for meaning in the dying Austro-Hungarian Empire.
books.historical_context: Set before WWI, examining the intellectual crisis of modern Europe.

books.title: Treasure Island
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: Young Jim Hawkins' adventure with pirates searching for buried treasure.
books.historical_context: A classic adventure novel that defined the pirate genre in popular culture.

books.title: The Long Goodbye
books.author: Raymond Chandler
books.summary: Private detective Philip Marlowe investigates a friend's alleged murder.
books.historical_context: Chandler's masterpiece of hard-boiled detective fiction, defining noir literature.

books.title: The Tin Drum
books.author: Günter Grass
books.summary: Oskar Matzerath refuses to grow up, witnessing the rise of Nazi Germany.
books.historical_context: A magical realist examination of German guilt and the Nazi period.

books.title: The Second Sex
books.author: Simone de Beauvoir
books.summary: A foundational feminist text analyzing women's oppression throughout history.
books.historical_context: Written after WWII, launching second-wave feminism.

books.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.summary: Sherlock Holmes investigates a supernatural hound terrorizing the Baskerville family.
books.historical_context: The most famous Sherlock Holmes novel, blending detective fiction with Gothic horror.

books.title: Dead Souls
books.author: Nikolai Gogol
books.summary: Chichikov's scheme to buy the legal rights to deceased serfs.
books.historical_context: A satirical portrait of Russian serfdom and bureaucracy.

books.title: The Waste Land
books.author: T. S. Eliot
books.summary: A fragmented poem depicting spiritual desolation in the modern world.
books.historical_context: A landmark of modernist poetry reflecting post-WWI disillusionment.

books.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
books.author: Patricia Highsmith
books.summary: Tom Ripley's murderous identity theft and psychological manipulation.
books.historical_context: A psychological thriller exploring post-war American ambition and moral corruption.

books.title: The Wind in the Willows
books.author: Kenneth Grahame
books.summary: The adventures of Mole, Rat, Badger, and the irrepressible Mr. Toad.
books.historical_context: An Edwardian children's classic celebrating friendship and the English countryside.

books.title: Brideshead Revisited
books.author: Evelyn Waugh
books.summary: Charles Ryder's relationship with the aristocratic Flyte family and their Catholic faith.
books.historical_context: Written during WWII, mourning the end of English aristocratic culture.

books.title: The Big Sleep
books.author: Raymond Chandler
books.summary: Philip Marlowe investigates a blackmail case in corrupt 1940s Los Angeles.
books.historical_context: Chandler's debut novel established the hard-boiled detective archetype.

books.title: Bleak House
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: The interminable Jarndyce and Jarndyce lawsuit destroys lives across generations.
books.historical_context: Dickens' attack on the English legal system and social reform.

books.title: A Farewell to Arms
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.summary: An American ambulance driver's love affair during World War I.
books.historical_context: Hemingway's semi-autobiographical account of WWI and the 'Lost Generation.'

books.title: Antigone
books.author: Sophocles
books.summary: Antigone defies King Creon's order to leave her brother's body unburied.
books.historical_context: A Greek tragedy exploring the conflict between divine law and human authority.

books.title: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter
books.author: Carson McCullers
books.summary: A deaf-mute man becomes the confidant for various lonely townspeople.
books.historical_context: Set in the Depression-era South, exploring isolation and communication.

books.title: The Maltese Falcon
books.author: Dashiell Hammett
books.summary: Private detective Sam Spade hunts for a valuable bird statue.
books.historical_context: A foundational hard-boiled detective novel that influenced film noir.

books.title: Pedro Páramo
books.author: Juan Rulfo
books.summary: A man searches for his father in a ghost town populated by the dead.
books.historical_context: A masterpiece of magical realism exploring Mexican identity and the aftermath of revolution.

books.title: A Clockwork Orange
books.author: Anthony Burgess
books.summary: Alex's ultraviolent youth and subsequent psychological conditioning.
books.historical_context: Written during Cold War tensions, exploring free will versus social control.

books.title: The Tale of Genji
books.author: Murasaki Shikibu
books.summary: The romantic adventures of Prince Genji in the Japanese imperial court.
books.historical_context: Often considered the world's first novel, depicting Heian period court culture.

books.title: The Canterbury Tales
books.author: Geoffrey Chaucer
books.summary: Pilgrims tell stories on their journey to Canterbury Cathedral.
books.historical_context: A vivid portrait of medieval English society across all social classes.

books.title: King Lear
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: An aging king divides his kingdom among his daughters with tragic consequences.
books.historical_context: Shakespeare's darkest tragedy, exploring power, family, and justice.

books.title: Paradise Lost
books.author: John Milton
books.summary: The fall of Satan and the temptation of Adam and Eve.
books.historical_context: Written after the English Civil War, exploring rebellion, authority, and redemption.

books.title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.summary: Robert Jordan's mission to blow up a bridge during the Spanish Civil War.
books.historical_context: Hemingway's account of the Spanish Civil War and anti-fascist resistance.

books.title: The Name of the Rose
books.author: Umberto Eco
books.summary: A murder mystery set in a 14th-century monastery, blending philosophy and detection.
books.historical_context: A postmodern novel exploring knowledge, truth, and medieval theology.

books.title: The Unbearable Lightness of Being
books.author: Milan Kundera
books.summary: Four characters navigate love and politics during the Prague Spring.
books.historical_context: Set during the 1968 Soviet invasion, exploring freedom and meaninglessness.

books.title: Tess of the d'Urbervilles
books.author: Thomas Hardy
books.summary: Tess Durbeyfield's tragic life in rural England, exploring fate and social injustice.
books.historical_context: Hardy's critique of Victorian sexual morality and social class.

books.title: Memoirs of Hadrian
books.author: Marguerite Yourcenar
books.summary: Emperor Hadrian reflects on his life and the nature of power and mortality.
books.historical_context: A meditation on leadership and civilization through the lens of ancient Rome.

books.title: Doctor Faustus
books.author: Thomas Mann
books.summary: A composer's pact with the devil parallels Germany's descent into fascism.
books.historical_context: Mann's allegory for German intellectual and moral corruption leading to Nazism.

books.title: Wide Sargasso Sea
books.author: Jean Rhys
books.summary: The story of Bertha Mason, the 'madwoman in the attic' from Jane Eyre.
books.historical_context: A postcolonial response to Charlotte Brontë, exploring Caribbean identity and colonialism.

books.title: Lady Chatterley's Lover
books.author: D. H. Lawrence
books.summary: Lady Chatterley's affair with her gamekeeper challenges class and sexual conventions.
books.historical_context: A banned book exploring sexuality, class, and industrial alienation.

books.title: Macbeth
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: A Scottish general's bloody path to the throne, driven by ambition and prophecy.
books.historical_context: Written for King James I, exploring the corrupting nature of power.

books.title: The Woman in White
books.author: Wilkie Collins
books.summary: A mystery involving identity theft, madness, and a woman in white.
books.historical_context: One of the first detective novels, influencing the mystery genre.

books.title: Doctor Zhivago
books.author: Boris Pasternak
books.summary: Yuri Zhivago's life during the Russian Revolution and Civil War.
books.historical_context: Banned in the Soviet Union, depicting the Revolution's impact on individuals.

books.title: The Interpretation of Dreams
books.author: Sigmund Freud
books.summary: Freud's groundbreaking analysis of dreams and the unconscious mind.
books.historical_context: A foundational text of psychoanalysis that revolutionized psychology.

books.title: One Day in the Life of Ivan Denisovich
books.author: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
books.summary: A single day in the life of a Soviet political prisoner.
books.historical_context: The first published work exposing Stalin's labor camps to the Soviet public.

books.title: Poems of Emily Dickinson
books.author: Emily Dickinson
books.summary: Innovative poems exploring death, immortality, and nature.
books.historical_context: Published posthumously, revolutionizing American poetry with unique style.

books.title: If This Is a Man
books.author: Primo Levi
books.summary: Levi's firsthand account of survival in Auschwitz concentration camp.
books.historical_context: A crucial Holocaust testimony examining the dehumanization of Nazi camps.

books.title: Sons and Lovers
books.author: D. H. Lawrence
books.summary: Paul Morel's relationship with his mother and lovers in working-class England.
books.historical_context: A semi-autobiographical novel exploring Oedipal relationships and industrial society.

books.title: Tom Jones
books.author: Henry Fielding
books.summary: The foundling Tom Jones's picaresque adventures in 18th-century England.
books.historical_context: A comic epic that helped establish the English novel form.

books.title: The Charterhouse of Parma
books.author: Stendhal
books.summary: Fabrizio del Dongo's adventures in post-Napoleonic Italy.
books.historical_context: A psychological novel examining passion and politics in 19th-century Italy.

books.title: A Sentimental Education
books.author: Gustave Flaubert
books.summary: Frédéric Moreau's unrequited love and political awakening in Paris.
books.historical_context: Set during the 1848 Revolution, examining romantic and political disillusionment.

books.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
books.author: Douglas Adams
books.summary: Arthur Dent's cosmic adventures after Earth's destruction by aliens.
books.historical_context: A science fiction comedy that became a cultural phenomenon.

books.title: The Three Musketeers
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.summary: D'Artagnan joins the king's musketeers in 17th-century France.
books.historical_context: A swashbuckling adventure set during the reign of Louis XIII.

books.title: Giovanni's Room
books.author: James Baldwin
books.summary: An American expatriate's affair with an Italian bartender in Paris.
books.historical_context: A groundbreaking exploration of homosexuality and identity in the 1950s.

books.title: Howards End
books.author: E. M. Forster
books.summary: The connection between the intellectual Schlegel sisters and the business-minded Wilcox family.
books.historical_context: Forster's examination of class, culture, and social change in Edwardian England.

books.title: And Then There Were None
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.summary: Ten strangers are trapped on an island where they're murdered one by one.
books.historical_context: Christie's masterpiece of the 'closed room' mystery genre.

books.title: A Room of One's Own
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.summary: Woolf's essay on women and fiction, arguing for women's intellectual independence.
books.historical_context: A foundational feminist text examining women's exclusion from literary tradition.

books.title: Fahrenheit 451
books.author: Ray Bradbury
books.summary: Guy Montag rebels against a society that burns books.
books.historical_context: Written during McCarthyism, warning against censorship and conformity.

books.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
books.author: Ken Kesey
books.summary: Randle McMurphy's rebellion against the oppressive mental hospital system.
books.historical_context: A critique of institutional authority and conformity in 1960s America.

books.title: Tender Is the Night
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.summary: Dick and Nicole Diver's marriage deteriorates amid the glamour of the Riviera.
books.historical_context: Fitzgerald's exploration of wealth, mental illness, and the American expatriate experience.

books.title: The Prince
books.author: Niccolò Machiavelli
books.summary: A treatise on political power and leadership, advocating pragmatic rule.
books.historical_context: Written during Italian Renaissance political turmoil, influencing political thought.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
books.author: J.K. Rowling
books.summary: Harry Potter discovers he's a wizard and attends Hogwarts School.
books.historical_context: A modern fantasy that revitalized children's literature and reading culture.

books.title: Steppenwolf
books.author: Hermann Hesse
books.summary: Harry Haller's spiritual crisis and journey toward self-acceptance.
books.historical_context: Written during the Weimar Republic, exploring alienation and spiritual seeking.

books.title: Love in the Time of Cholera
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.summary: Florentino Ariza's lifelong love for Fermina Daza spans decades.
books.historical_context: Márquez's exploration of love, aging, and Latin American society.

books.title: The Complete Sherlock Holmes
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.summary: The complete adventures of the world's most famous consulting detective.
books.historical_context: Doyle created the template for the modern detective story in Victorian London.

books.title: Confessions of Zeno
books.author: Italo Svevo
books.summary: Zeno Cosini's humorous and self-deceptive memoirs.
books.historical_context: An early modernist novel exploring psychoanalysis and self-awareness.

books.title: Light in August
books.author: William Faulkner
books.summary: Joe Christmas's violent life intersects with other characters in the South.
books.historical_context: Faulkner's exploration of race, violence, and identity in the American South.

books.title: Decameron
books.author: Giovanni Boccaccio
books.summary: One hundred tales told by ten young people fleeing the Black Death.
books.historical_context: A masterpiece of medieval literature reflecting 14th-century Italian society.

books.title: Lord Jim
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: Jim's quest for redemption after abandoning a ship in crisis.
books.historical_context: Conrad's examination of honor, guilt, and colonialism in the East Indies.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.summary: Bilbo Baggins's unexpected adventure with dwarves and a dragon.
books.historical_context: Tolkien's children's fantasy that laid the groundwork for modern fantasy literature.

books.title: Rabbit, Run
books.author: John Updike
books.summary: Harry 'Rabbit' Angstrom flees his suburban responsibilities.
books.historical_context: Updike's portrayal of middle-class American life and masculinity in crisis.

books.title: Man's Fate
books.author: André Malraux
books.summary: Communist revolutionaries face defeat in 1927 Shanghai.
books.historical_context: Malraux's existential novel set during the Chinese Communist uprising.

books.title: The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie
books.author: Muriel Spark
books.summary: An unconventional teacher's influence on her students in 1930s Edinburgh.
books.historical_context: Spark's exploration of education, fascism, and individual versus group loyalty.

books.title: Confessions
books.author: Augustine
books.summary: Augustine's spiritual autobiography and conversion to Christianity.
books.historical_context: A foundational Christian text that influenced Western autobiography and theology.

books.title: White Teeth
books.author: Zadie Smith
books.summary: Two families navigate multicultural London across generations.
books.historical_context: A contemporary novel exploring immigration, identity, and cultural collision.

books.title: The Spy Who Came in from the Cold
books.author: John le Carré
books.summary: Alec Leamas undertakes a final mission in Cold War Berlin.
books.historical_context: Le Carré's realistic portrayal of espionage during the Cold War.

books.title: Metamorphoses
books.author: Ovid
books.summary: A narrative poem chronicling transformations from Greek and Roman mythology.
books.historical_context: Ovid's masterwork influencing Western literature's treatment of mythology.

books.title: A Good Man Is Hard to Find
books.author: Flannery O'Connor
books.summary: Dark Southern Gothic stories exploring grace, violence, and redemption.
books.historical_context: O'Connor's Catholic perspective on the Protestant South during the Civil Rights era.

books.title: Winnie-the-Pooh
books.author: A.A. Milne
books.summary: Christopher Robin and his toy bear's gentle adventures in the Hundred Acre Wood.
books.historical_context: A beloved children's classic that captured post-WWI nostalgia for innocence.

books.title: The Flowers of Evil
books.author: Charles Baudelaire
books.summary: Poems exploring urban life, decay, and the relationship between beauty and evil.
books.historical_context: Baudelaire's scandalous collection pioneered modern poetry and symbolism.

books.title: Brighton Rock
books.author: Graham Greene
books.summary: Teenage gangster Pinkie Brown's violent career in seaside Brighton.
books.historical_context: Greene's exploration of Catholic themes and moral corruption in interwar Britain.

books.title: The Corrections
books.author: Jonathan Franzen
books.summary: The dysfunctional Lambert family struggles with aging, marriage, and modern life.
books.historical_context: A contemporary family saga exploring American middle-class anxieties.

books.title: On the Origin of Species
books.author: Charles Darwin
books.summary: Darwin's theory of evolution by natural selection.
books.historical_context: A revolutionary scientific work that transformed biological understanding.

books.title: Of Mice and Men
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.summary: George and Lennie's friendship and tragic end during the Great Depression.
books.historical_context: Steinbeck's portrayal of migrant workers and the American Dream's failure.

books.title: Hans Christian Andersen's Fairy Tales
books.author: Hans Christian Andersen
books.summary: Beloved fairy tales including 'The Little Mermaid' and 'The Ugly Duckling.'
books.historical_context: Andersen revolutionized children's literature with psychologically complex tales.

books.title: A Wrinkle in Time
books.author: Madeleine L'Engle
books.summary: Meg Murry travels through space and time to rescue her father.
books.historical_context: A science fantasy that challenged children's literature conventions.

books.title: The End of the Affair
books.author: Graham Greene
books.summary: A love triangle complicated by faith and jealousy during WWII London.
books.historical_context: Greene's exploration of love, faith, and divine intervention during wartime.

books.title: Molloy
books.author: Samuel Beckett
books.summary: Two narrators, Molloy and Moran, embark on mysterious quests.
books.historical_context: Part of Beckett's trilogy exploring post-war existential uncertainty.

books.title: The Remains of the Day
books.author: Kazuo Ishiguro
books.summary: Butler Stevens reflects on his life of service and repressed emotions.
books.historical_context: Ishiguro's examination of duty, dignity, and the cost of emotional repression.

books.title: Portnoy's Complaint
books.author: Philip Roth
books.summary: Alexander Portnoy's sexual confessions and neuroses to his psychiatrist.
books.historical_context: Roth's controversial exploration of Jewish-American identity and sexuality.

books.title: An American Tragedy
books.author: Theodore Dreiser
books.summary: Clyde Griffiths's rise and fall in pursuit of the American Dream.
books.historical_context: Dreiser's naturalistic novel critiquing American society and capitalism.

books.title: The Good Soldier Švejk
books.author: Jaroslav Hašek
books.summary: The bumbling soldier Švejk's subversive adventures during WWI.
books.historical_context: A satirical antiwar novel exposing the absurdity of military bureaucracy.

books.title: Watership Down
books.author: Richard Adams
books.summary: A group of rabbits search for a new home after their warren is destroyed.
books.historical_context: An allegorical adventure story that became a classic of children's literature.

books.title: The Counterfeiters
books.author: André Gide
books.summary: A complex novel exploring authenticity, morality, and artistic creation.
books.historical_context: Gide's experimental novel challenging traditional narrative forms.

books.title: Fathers and Sons
books.author: Ivan Turgenev
books.summary: The generational conflict between nihilist Bazarov and traditional Russian society.
books.historical_context: Turgenev's portrayal of the ideological divide in 1860s Russia.

books.title: Atonement
books.author: Ian McEwan
books.summary: Briony Tallis's false accusation destroys lives and haunts her forever.
books.historical_context: A contemporary novel exploring guilt, memory, and the power of narrative.

books.title: Berlin Alexanderplatz
books.author: Alfred Döblin
books.summary: Franz Biberkopf's struggle to stay honest in Weimar Republic Berlin.
books.historical_context: A modernist novel capturing the chaos and vitality of 1920s Berlin.

books.title: My Ántonia
books.author: Willa Cather
books.summary: Jim Burden's memories of the pioneering Bohemian girl Ántonia.
books.historical_context: Cather's celebration of immigrant experiences on the American frontier.

books.title: Gargantua and Pantagruel
books.author: François Rabelais
books.summary: The comic adventures of two giants, celebrating humanist values.
books.historical_context: Rabelais's Renaissance satire celebrating learning, pleasure, and human nature.

books.title: A Tale of Two Cities
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: Sydney Carton's redemption during the French Revolution.
books.historical_context: Dickens's tale of sacrifice and resurrection set during revolutionary upheaval.

books.title: A House for Mr Biswas
books.author: V.S. Naipaul
books.summary: Mohun Biswas's lifelong quest for independence and a house of his own.
books.historical_context: Naipaul's portrayal of Indo-Caribbean life and post-colonial identity.

books.title: The Once and Future King
books.author: T.H. White
books.summary: The complete story of King Arthur from childhood to death.
books.historical_context: White's modern retelling of Arthurian legend, reflecting on war and civilization.

books.title: U.S.A. Trilogy
books.author: John Dos Passos
books.summary: A panoramic view of American society from 1900 to 1930.
books.historical_context: Dos Passos's experimental trilogy capturing American social and political upheaval.

books.title: The Communist Manifesto
books.author: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels
books.summary: A political pamphlet outlining the theory and program of communism.
books.historical_context: A revolutionary text that influenced political movements worldwide.

books.title: The Autobiography of Malcolm X
books.author: Malcolm X and Alex Haley
books.summary: Malcolm X's transformation from criminal to civil rights leader.
books.historical_context: A powerful account of racial awakening during the Civil Rights Movement.

books.title: All the King's Men
books.author: Robert Penn Warren
books.summary: The rise and fall of populist politician Willie Stark.
books.historical_context: Warren's exploration of political corruption and moral compromise.

books.title: Never Let Me Go
books.author: Kazuo Ishiguro
books.summary: Students at a mysterious boarding school discover their tragic destiny.
books.historical_context: Ishiguro's dystopian meditation on humanity, memory, and mortality.

books.title: Bonjour Tristesse
books.author: Françoise Sagan
books.summary: Seventeen-year-old Cécile's summer of love, jealousy, and tragedy.
books.historical_context: Sagan's precocious debut exploring youth, sexuality, and moral awakening.

books.title: A Confederacy of Dunces
books.author: John Kennedy Toole
books.summary: Ignatius J. Reilly's misadventures in New Orleans society.
books.historical_context: A posthumously published comic masterpiece of American eccentricity.

books.title: Blood Meridian
books.author: Cormac McCarthy
books.summary: The Kid's journey with a violent gang of scalp hunters.
books.Historical_context: McCarthy's brutal examination of violence and the American frontier.

books.title: A Christmas Carol
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: Ebenezer Scrooge's Christmas Eve transformation through ghostly visitations.
books.historical_context: Dickens's beloved tale that helped define modern Christmas traditions.

books.title: Anne of Green Gables
books.author: L.M. Montgomery
books.summary: Orphan Anne Shirley finds a home with the Cuthberts on Prince Edward Island.
books.Historical_context: Montgomery's celebration of imagination, nature, and Maritime Canadian life.
